pubmed_links: [Williamson et al. (2014) Heterozygous loss-of-function mutations in YAP1 cause coloboma with or without intellectual disability](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24462371/)
summary: Heterozygous loss-of-function variants in YAP1 cause a syndrome including ocular coloboma, cleft lip/palate, and intellectual disability; the Hippo/YAP pathway is also implicated in cortical development and neural stem cell regulation. (emerging evidence)
